---
title: "Received Funding: National Parks & Nature Reserves"
author: "Erlangga"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: columns
    vertical_layout: fill
    theme:
      version: 4
      bootswatch: flatly
---

<style>
.navbar { display: none; }
body { padding-top: 0 !important; }
</style>

```{r}
# Install/load necessary packages
if (!require("dplyr")) install.packages("dplyr")
if (!require("stringr")) install.packages("stringr")
if (!require("viridis")) install.packages("viridis")
if (!require("leaflet")) install.packages("leaflet")
if (!require("leaflet.extras")) install.packages("leaflet.extras")
if (!require("leafem")) install.packages("leafem") # for minimap
if (!require("htmltools")) install.packages("htmltools")
if (!require("htmlwidgets")) install.packages("htmlwidgets")
if (!require("maps")) install.packages("maps")

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(viridis)
library(leaflet)
library(leaflet.extras)
library(leafem)
library(htmltools)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(sf)
library(maps)

# 1. Load Myanmar eco data
sites <- read.csv("Funded_PA_Final_Myanmar.csv", check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, fileEncoding = "UTF-8")
sites <- sites[, !is.na(names(sites)) & names(sites) != ""]
sites <- sites[, !duplicated(names(sites))]

# Load WDPA Shapefiles
shp0 <- st_read("WDPA_WDOECM_Jun2026_Public_MMR_shp_0/WDPA_WDOECM_Jun2026_Public_MMR_shp-polygons.shp", quiet = TRUE)
shp1 <- st_read("WDPA_WDOECM_Jun2026_Public_MMR_shp_1/WDPA_WDOECM_Jun2026_Public_MMR_shp-polygons.shp", quiet = TRUE)
shp2 <- st_read("WDPA_WDOECM_Jun2026_Public_MMR_shp_2/WDPA_WDOECM_Jun2026_Public_MMR_shp-polygons.shp", quiet = TRUE)

# Combine and transform to WGS84 for Leaflet
wdpa_polygons <- rbind(shp0, shp1, shp2)
wdpa_polygons <- st_transform(wdpa_polygons, 4326)

# Clip polygons that extend beyond Myanmar boundary
myanmar_map <- map("world", region = "Myanmar", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE)
myanmar_sf <- st_as_sf(myanmar_map)
st_crs(myanmar_sf) <- 4326
myanmar_sf <- st_make_valid(myanmar_sf)

wdpa_polygons <- st_make_valid(wdpa_polygons)
suppressMessages(sf_use_s2(FALSE))
wdpa_polygons <- suppressWarnings(st_intersection(wdpa_polygons, myanmar_sf))
suppressMessages(sf_use_s2(TRUE))

# Prepare safe popup for WDPA polygons
wdpa_popup <- paste0(
  "<div style='font-size: 9px; line-height: 1.4;'>",
  "<b style='font-size: 10px;'>", if("NAME" %in% names(wdpa_polygons)) wdpa_polygons$NAME else "WDPA Area", "</b><br/>",
  "<b>Designation:</b> ", if("DESIG_ENG" %in% names(wdpa_polygons)) wdpa_polygons$DESIG_ENG else "Unknown", "<br/>",
  "<b>Status:</b> ", if("STATUS" %in% names(wdpa_polygons)) wdpa_polygons$STATUS else "Unknown",
  "</div>"
)

# 2. Filter out rows with missing coordinates
sites <- sites %>%
  filter(!is.na(longitude) & !is.na(latitude))

# 3. Clean and prepare data for mapping
sites$name <- str_trim(sites$name)
sites$`Status Area` <- str_to_title(str_trim(sites$`Status Area`))
sites$`Status Area` <- gsub("\\bOf\\b", "of", sites$`Status Area`)
sites$`Status Area` <- gsub(" And ", "; ", sites$`Status Area`)

# Count duplicates — uniform size, label shows exact count
sites <- sites %>%
  group_by(longitude, latitude) %>%
  mutate(loc_count = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(point_radius = 8)  # fixed uniform radius for all markers

# Helper function to generate multiple links
generate_multiple_links <- function(info_str) {
  if (is.na(info_str) || info_str == "") return("No website available")
  
  links <- unlist(strsplit(info_str, "[;\n]+"))
  links <- stringr::str_trim(links)
  links <- links[links != ""]
  
  if (length(links) == 0) return("No website available")
  
  a_tags <- paste0('<a href="', links, '" target="_blank">Link ', seq_along(links), '</a>')
  return(paste(a_tags, collapse = " | "))
}

# 4. Create clickable links for the website
sites <- sites %>%
  mutate(
    TautanLink = sapply(`More Info`, generate_multiple_links)
  )

# 5. Create color palette for categorical data
all_status <- unique(sites$`Status Area`)
all_status <- sort(all_status[!is.na(all_status) & all_status != ""])

# Define custom color mapping
status_color_mapping <- c(
  "National Park" = "#88CCEE",
  "National Park; Asean Heritage Park" = "#E69F00",
  "Wetland of International Importance (Ramsar Site)" = "#882255",
  "Wetland of International Importance (Ramsar Site); Wildlife Sanctuary; Asean Heritage Park" = "#A6D854",
  "Wildlife Sanctuary" = "#8C510A",
  "Wildlife Sanctuary; Asean Heritage Park" = "#2c4875"
)

assigned_colors <- unname(status_color_mapping[all_status])
assigned_colors[is.na(assigned_colors)] <- "#808080" # Fallback

pal <- colorFactor(palette = assigned_colors, domain = all_status, na.color = "#808080")

# 6. Create HTML popup labels with formatted table
sites <- sites %>%
  mutate(
    popup = paste0(
      "<div style='font-size: 9px; line-height: 1.5; font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>",
      "<div style='background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1a5276, #2980b9); color: #fff; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 6px 6px 0 0; margin: -14px -14px 10px -14px;'>",
      "<b style='font-size: 11px;'>", name, "</b><br/>",
      "<span style='font-size: 9px; opacity: 0.9;'>", `Status Area`, "</span>",
      "</div>",
      "<table style='width:100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin-bottom: 10px;'>",
      "<tr style='background:#f8f9fa;'><th style='padding:6px 8px; text-align:left; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6; color:#495057; width:35%;'>Type of Financing</th><td style='padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6;'>", `Type of Financing`, "</td></tr>",
      "<tr><th style='padding:6px 8px; text-align:left; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6; color:#495057;'>Fund Provider</th><td style='padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6;'>", `Fund Provider`, "</td></tr>",
      "<tr style='background:#f8f9fa;'><th style='padding:6px 8px; text-align:left; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6; color:#495057;'>Intermediaries [Roles]</th><td style='padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6;'>", `Intermediaries [Roles]`, "</td></tr>",
      "<tr><th style='padding:6px 8px; text-align:left; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6; color:#495057;'>Beneficiaries</th><td style='padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6;'>", Beneficiaries, "</td></tr>",
      "<tr style='background:#f8f9fa;'><th style='padding:6px 8px; text-align:left; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6; color:#495057;'>Focussed Area</th><td style='padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6;'>", `Focussed Area`, "</td></tr>",
      "<tr><th style='padding:6px 8px; text-align:left; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6; color:#495057;'>Duration</th><td style='padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6;'>", Duration, "</td></tr>",
      "<tr style='background:#f8f9fa;'><th style='padding:6px 8px; text-align:left; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6; color:#495057;'>Commodity</th><td style='padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6;'>", `Commodity [solution-oriented]`, "</td></tr>",
      "<tr><th style='padding:6px 8px; text-align:left; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6; color:#495057;'>Ecosystem Services</th><td style='padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid #dee2e6;'>", `Ecosystem Services`, "</td></tr>",
      "</table>",
      "<div style='padding: 4px 0; border-top: 1px solid #eee;'>",
      "<b style='color:#555;'>&#128279; References:</b><br/>", TautanLink, 
      "</div>",
      "</div>"
    )
  )

# Function to create an interactive HTML carousel/slider for duplicated popups
make_slides <- function(popups, lon, lat) {
  n <- length(popups)
  if (n == 1) return(popups[1])
  
  uid <- paste0(abs(round(lon[1]*10000)), "_", abs(round(lat[1]*10000)), "_", sample(1000:9999, 1))
  
  slides <- character(n)
  for (i in 1:n) {
    display_style <- if(i == 1) "display:block;" else "display:none;"
    
    btn_prev <- if(i > 1) paste0("<button style='margin-right:5px; padding: 4px 12px; font-size: 10px; cursor:pointer; background:#2980b9; color:#fff; border:none; border-radius:4px;' onclick=\"document.getElementById('slide_", uid, "_", i, "').style.display='none'; document.getElementById('slide_", uid, "_", i-1, "').style.display='block';\">&laquo; Prev</button>") else ""
    btn_next <- if(i < n) paste0("<button style='padding: 4px 12px; font-size: 10px; cursor:pointer; background:#2980b9; color:#fff; border:none; border-radius:4px;' onclick=\"document.getElementById('slide_", uid, "_", i, "').style.display='none'; document.getElementById('slide_", uid, "_", i+1, "').style.display='block';\">Next &raquo;</button>") else ""
    
    nav_html <- paste0("<div style='text-align:center; margin-top:15px; padding-top:10px; border-top:2px solid #2980b9;'>", 
                       "<span style='font-size:10px; color:#2980b9;'><b>Funding Record ", i, " of ", n, "</b></span><br/><div style='margin-top:5px;'>",
                       btn_prev, btn_next, 
                       "</div></div>")
                       
    slides[i] <- paste0("<div id='slide_", uid, "_", i, "' style='", display_style, "'>", popups[i], nav_html, "</div>")
  }
  
  return(paste0("<div id='carousel_", uid, "'>", paste(slides, collapse=""), "</div>"))
}

# Combine popups for duplicate locations so they can be viewed via slides
sites_leaflet <- sites %>%
  group_by(longitude, latitude, `Status Area`, point_radius) %>%
  summarize(
    popup    = make_slides(popup, longitude, latitude),
    loc_count = first(loc_count),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 7. Create Leaflet interactive map
m <- leaflet(sites_leaflet) %>%
  
  # --- ADD BASEMAPS ---
  addProviderTiles(providers$OpenStreetMap, group = "OpenStreetMap") %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$Esri.WorldImagery, group = "Satellite") %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$Esri.WorldTopoMap, group = "Terrain") %>%
  
  # --- ADD WDPA POLYGONS ---
  addPolygons(
    data = wdpa_polygons,
    fillColor = "forestgreen",
    fillOpacity = 0.2,
    color = "darkgreen",
    weight = 1.5,
    group = "WDPA Polygons",
    popup = wdpa_popup,
    popupOptions = popupOptions(maxWidth = 400, closeOnClick = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude, lat = ~latitude,
    radius = ~point_radius,   # uniform radius = 8 px for all
    color = "black",
    weight = 1.5,
    fillColor = ~pal(`Status Area`),
    fillOpacity = 0.85,
    group = "Funded",
    popup = ~lapply(popup, HTML),
    popupOptions = popupOptions(maxWidth = 700, minWidth = 500, closeOnClick = TRUE, autoClose = TRUE)
  ) %>%

  # Overlay exact funding-count labels centred inside each circle
  addLabelOnlyMarkers(
    data = sites_leaflet,
    lng = ~longitude, lat = ~latitude,
    label = ~as.character(loc_count),
    group = "Funded",
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      permanent   = TRUE,
      direction   = "center",
      textOnly    = TRUE,
      style = list(
        "color"       = "white",
        "font-weight" = "bold",
        "font-size"   = "11px",
        "text-shadow" = "0 0 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.7)"
      )
    )
  ) %>%

  addLegend(
    pal = pal,
    values = all_status,
    title = "Status Area",
    position = "bottomright",
    opacity = 0.8,
    na.label = "Unknown"
  ) %>%
  addControl(
    html = "<div style='background: white; padding: 10px; border-radius: 6px; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 5px;'>
      <strong style='font-size: 13px; margin-bottom: 8px; display: block; color: #333;'>Number of Fundings</strong>
      <div style='display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 4px;'>
        <div style='width: 28px; height: 28px; border-radius: 50%; background: #555; border: 1.5px solid #333; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0;'>
          <span style='color: white; font-size: 11px; font-weight: bold;'>n</span>
        </div>
        <span style='font-size: 12px; color: #333;'>= number of funding in the PA </span>
      </div>
    </div>",
    position = "bottomright"
  ) %>%
  addMiniMap(
    tiles = providers$OpenStreetMap,
    toggleDisplay = TRUE,
    position = "bottomleft"
  ) %>%
  addMouseCoordinates() %>%
  
  # --- ADD LAYER CONTROL CHECKLIST ---
  addLayersControl(
    baseGroups = c("OpenStreetMap", "Satellite", "Terrain"),
    overlayGroups = c("WDPA Polygons", "Funded"),
    options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE)
  )

# 8. Print map in R viewer or R Markdown
m

# 9. Save as an interactive standalone HTML file
saveWidget(m, file = "funded_PA_map.html", selfcontained = TRUE, libdir = "funded_PA_lib")
```

```{r}
# ── 1. Install / load packages ────────────────────────────────────────────────
if (!require("sf"))           install.packages("sf")
if (!require("readr"))        install.packages("readr")
if (!require("dplyr"))        install.packages("dplyr")
if (!require("maps"))         install.packages("maps")
if (!require("stringr"))      install.packages("stringr")
if (!require("viridis"))      install.packages("viridis")

library(sf)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(maps)
library(stringr)
library(viridis)

# =============================================================================
# 2. Read Eco-Tourism data
# =============================================================================
funded_only <- read.csv("Funded_PA_Final_Myanmar.csv", 
                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE, 
                        check.names = FALSE,
                        fileEncoding = "UTF-8")
funded_only <- funded_only[, !is.na(names(funded_only)) & names(funded_only) != ""]
funded_only <- funded_only[, !duplicated(names(funded_only))]

# Clean and trim strings
funded_only$name <- str_trim(funded_only$name)
funded_only$`Status Area` <- str_to_title(str_trim(funded_only$`Status Area`))
funded_only$`Status Area` <- gsub("\\bOf\\b", "of", funded_only$`Status Area`)
funded_only$`Status Area` <- gsub(" And ", "; ", funded_only$`Status Area`)

# Remove rows with missing coordinates or empty status area
funded_clean <- funded_only %>%
  filter(!is.na(longitude) & !is.na(latitude) & !is.na(`Status Area`) & `Status Area` != "")

# Extract levels from dataset
all_status_static <- unique(funded_clean$`Status Area`)
all_status_static <- sort(all_status_static[!is.na(all_status_static) & all_status_static != ""])

# Convert Status Area to factor for categorical color mapping
funded_clean$`Status Area` <- factor(funded_clean$`Status Area`, levels = all_status_static)

# Count duplicates — uniform size, label shows exact count
funded_clean <- funded_clean %>%
  group_by(longitude, latitude) %>%
  mutate(loc_count = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(point_cex = 1.2)  # fixed uniform size for all markers

# Deduplicated coordinates for label placement (one label per unique location)
funded_unique <- funded_clean %>%
  group_by(longitude, latitude) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()

# Exact coordinates
coords_funded <- data.frame(lon = funded_clean$longitude, lat = funded_clean$latitude)

# =============================================================================
# 3. Colour scale -- mapping based on Status Area
# =============================================================================
# Extract unique levels and assign custom colors
status_levels <- levels(funded_clean$`Status Area`)
status_color_mapping <- c(
  "National Park" = "#88CCEE",
  "National Park; Asean Heritage Park" = "#E69F00",
  "Wetland of International Importance (Ramsar Site)" = "#882255",
  "Wetland of International Importance (Ramsar Site); Wildlife Sanctuary; Asean Heritage Park" = "#A6D854",
  "Wildlife Sanctuary" = "#8C510A",
  "Wildlife Sanctuary; Asean Heritage Park" = "#2c4875"
)

status_colors <- unname(status_color_mapping[status_levels])
status_colors[is.na(status_colors)] <- "#808080" # Fallback

# Assign colors to each point
point_colors_funded <- status_colors[as.numeric(funded_clean$`Status Area`)]

# =============================================================================
# 4. Map bounds (Adjusted for Myanmar)
# =============================================================================
min_lon <- 87; max_lon <- 107
min_lat <- 8;   max_lat <- 30

# =============================================================================
# 5. Open PDF device for Funded Status map
# =============================================================================
pdf("National_Parks_&_Nature_Reserves_Received_Funding_Supports_in_Myanmar.pdf", width = 8, height = 10)

old_par <- par(no.readonly = TRUE)
par(xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")

mean_lat <- mean(c(min_lat, max_lat))
aspect_ratio <- 1 / cos(mean_lat * pi / 180)

# Initialise blank plot
plot(NA,
     xlim = c(min_lon, max_lon),
     ylim = c(min_lat, max_lat),
     asp = aspect_ratio,
     type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", axes = FALSE)

# Ocean background
usr <- par("usr")
rect(usr[1], usr[3], usr[2], usr[4], col = "lightblue", border = NA)

# Land polygons (World - outside Myanmar)
maps::map("world",
    xlim = c(min_lon - 2, max_lon + 2), ylim = c(min_lat - 2, max_lat + 2),
    col = "white", fill = TRUE, add = TRUE)

# Myanmar polygon
maps::map("world", region = "Myanmar",
    xlim = c(min_lon - 2, max_lon + 2), ylim = c(min_lat - 2, max_lat + 2),
    col = "grey85", fill = TRUE, add = TRUE)

# Plot WDPA shapefile polygons
plot(st_geometry(wdpa_polygons), col = adjustcolor("forestgreen", alpha.f = 0.2), border = "darkgreen", lwd = 0.5, add = TRUE)

# Grid lines every 2 degrees
abline(v = seq(min_lon, max_lon, 2), col = "grey70", lty = 3)
abline(h = seq(min_lat, max_lat, 2), col = "grey70", lty = 3)

# Axes
axis(1, at = seq(min_lon, max_lon, 2), labels = paste0(seq(min_lon, max_lon, 2), "\u00b0E"), cex.axis = 0.7)
axis(2, at = seq(min_lat, max_lat, 2), labels = paste0(abs(seq(min_lat, max_lat, 2)), ifelse(seq(min_lat, max_lat, 2) < 0, "\u00b0S", "\u00b0N")), cex.axis = 0.7)

# Plot points — uniform size
points(coords_funded[, 1], coords_funded[, 2],
       pch = 21, bg = point_colors_funded, col = "black", cex = funded_clean$point_cex)

# Overlay exact funding-count labels centred inside each circle
text(funded_unique$longitude, funded_unique$latitude,
     labels = funded_unique$loc_count,
     cex = 0.45, col = "white", font = 2)

# Categorical Legend
legend("bottomleft", 
       legend = status_levels,
       pt.bg = status_colors, 
       pch = 22, col = "black", pt.cex = 0.9, # square box to indicate just color
       title = "Status Area",
       bg = "white", cex = 0.4875, inset = c(0.02, 0.05))

# Number of Fundings legend — circle with white "n" centred inside
leg_fund1 <- legend(
  "bottomleft",
  legend    = "= number of fundings in the PA",
  pt.bg     = "grey50",
  pch       = 21, col = "black", pt.cex = 1.2,
  title     = "Number of Fundings",
  bg        = "white", cex = 0.675,
  x.intersp = 1.2, y.intersp = 1.0,
  inset     = c(0.02, 0.265))

# Overlay white "n" centred inside the legend circle symbol
text(
  x      = (leg_fund1$rect$left + leg_fund1$text$x[1]) / 2,
  y      = leg_fund1$text$y[1],
  labels = "n",
  col    = "white", cex = 0.42, font = 2
)

# Scale bar & North arrow
scalebar_x <- max_lon - 2.5
scalebar_y <- min_lat + 0.5
rect(scalebar_x, scalebar_y, scalebar_x + 1.8, scalebar_y + 0.1, col = "black")
text(scalebar_x + 0.9, scalebar_y - 0.3, "200 km", cex = 0.7, adj = 0.5)

north_x <- max_lon - 0.5
north_y <- max_lat - 0.5
text(north_x, north_y, "N", cex = 1.1, font = 2)

# Map title
title(
  main = "National Parks & Nature Reserves Received Funding Supports in Myanmar",
  sub  = paste0("n = ", nrow(funded_clean), " sites"),
  cex.main = 1.05, font.main = 2, col.main = "grey10",
  cex.sub  = 0.8, col.sub  = "grey30"
)

# Add connected border around the map
box(lwd = 1.5)

par(old_par)
dev.off()

cat("\n✅ Static map created successfully!\n")
cat("📁 Map saved as: National_Parks_&_Nature_Reserves_Received_Funding_Supports_in_Myanmar.pdf\n")
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
if (!require("maptiles")) install.packages("maptiles")
if (!require("terra")) install.packages("terra")

library(maptiles)
library(terra)
library(sf)

# We use the same 'coords_funded', 'funded_clean', 'status_colors', 'point_colors_funded',
# 'min_lon', 'max_lon', 'min_lat', 'max_lat', 'status_levels' as above.

# Create a bounding box polygon for the Myanmar region to fetch tiles
bbox_myanmar <- st_bbox(c(xmin = min_lon, xmax = max_lon, ymin = min_lat, ymax = max_lat), crs = 4326)
myanmar_sfc <- st_as_sfc(bbox_myanmar)

# Fetch terrain tiles (Esri.WorldTopoMap)
# Zoom level 6 provides a good balance of detail and download size for the whole country
terrain_tiles <- get_tiles(x = myanmar_sfc, provider = "Esri.WorldTopoMap", zoom = 6, crop = TRUE, project = TRUE)

# Open PDF device
pdf("National Parks & Nature Reserves Received Funding Supports in Myanmar (Terrain).pdf", width = 8, height = 10)

old_par <- par(no.readonly = TRUE)
par(xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")

mean_lat <- mean(c(min_lat, max_lat))
aspect_ratio <- 1 / cos(mean_lat * pi / 180)

# Initialise blank plot
plot(NA,
     xlim = c(min_lon, max_lon),
     ylim = c(min_lat, max_lat),
     asp = aspect_ratio,
     type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", axes = FALSE)

# Plot the downloaded terrain tiles as the background
plot_tiles(terrain_tiles, add = TRUE)

# Draw Myanmar border in blue
maps::map("world", region = "Myanmar",
    xlim = c(min_lon - 2, max_lon + 2), ylim = c(min_lat - 2, max_lat + 2),
    col = "blue", fill = FALSE, add = TRUE, lwd = 2)

# Plot WDPA shapefile polygons
plot(st_geometry(wdpa_polygons), col = adjustcolor("forestgreen", alpha.f = 0.2), border = "darkgreen", lwd = 0.5, add = TRUE)

# Grid lines every 2 degrees
abline(v = seq(min_lon, max_lon, 2), col = "grey30", lty = 3)
abline(h = seq(min_lat, max_lat, 2), col = "grey30", lty = 3)

# Axes
axis(1, at = seq(min_lon, max_lon, 2), labels = paste0(seq(min_lon, max_lon, 2), "\u00b0E"), cex.axis = 0.7)
axis(2, at = seq(min_lat, max_lat, 2), labels = paste0(abs(seq(min_lat, max_lat, 2)), ifelse(seq(min_lat, max_lat, 2) < 0, "\u00b0S", "\u00b0N")), cex.axis = 0.7)

# Plot points — uniform size
points(coords_funded[, 1], coords_funded[, 2],
       pch = 21, bg = point_colors_funded, col = "black", cex = funded_clean$point_cex)

# Overlay exact funding-count labels centred inside each circle
text(funded_unique$longitude, funded_unique$latitude,
     labels = funded_unique$loc_count,
     cex = 0.45, col = "white", font = 2)

# Categorical Legend
legend("bottomleft", 
       legend = status_levels,
       pt.bg = status_colors, 
       pch = 22, col = "black", pt.cex = 0.9, # square box to indicate just color
       title = "Status Area",
       bg = "white", cex = 0.4875, inset = c(0.02, 0.05))

# Number of Fundings legend — circle with white "n" centred inside
leg_fund2 <- legend(
  "bottomleft",
  legend    = "= number of fundings in the PA",
  pt.bg     = "grey50",
  pch       = 21, col = "black", pt.cex = 1.2,
  title     = "Number of Fundings",
  bg        = "white", cex = 0.675,
  x.intersp = 1.2, y.intersp = 1.0,
  inset     = c(0.02, 0.265))

# Overlay white "n" centred inside the legend circle symbol
text(
  x      = (leg_fund2$rect$left + leg_fund2$text$x[1]) / 2,
  y      = leg_fund2$text$y[1],
  labels = "n",
  col    = "white", cex = 0.42, font = 2
)

# Scale bar & North arrow
scalebar_x <- max_lon - 2.5
scalebar_y <- min_lat + 0.5

# White background box for scalebar so it's readable over terrain
rect(scalebar_x, scalebar_y, scalebar_x + 1.8, scalebar_y + 0.15, col = "white", border = "black")
rect(scalebar_x, scalebar_y, scalebar_x + 0.9, scalebar_y + 0.15, col = "black")
text(scalebar_x + 0.9, scalebar_y - 0.3, "200 km", cex = 0.7, adj = 0.5, font = 2)

north_x <- max_lon - 0.5
north_y <- max_lat - 0.5
text(north_x, north_y, "N", cex = 1.1, font = 2)

# Map title
title(
  main = "National Parks & Nature Reserves Received Funding Supports in Myanmar (Terrain)",
  sub  = paste0("n = ", nrow(funded_clean), " sites"),
  cex.main = 1.05, font.main = 2, col.main = "grey10",
  cex.sub  = 0.8, col.sub  = "grey30"
)

# Add connected border around the map
box(lwd = 1.5)

par(old_par)
dev.off()

cat("\n✅ Terrain static map created successfully!\n")
cat("📁 Map saved as: National_Parks_&_Nature_Reserves_Received_Funding_Supports_in_Myanmar_Terrain.pdf\n")
```
